questions.statement:
<p><strong>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</strong></p>

<p>I hope that we will arrive at the ideal trade-off soon.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">In the following question, one part of the sentence may have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and click the button corresponding to it. If the sentence is free from error, click the &#39;No error&#39; option.</span></p>

<p>When there is an option between (A)/ the plastic and jute, the later is (B)/ doubtlessly the better&nbsp;choice. (C)/ No error (D)&nbsp;</p>

<p>Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</p>

<p>A soothe voice must convey both confidence and intimacy, qualities which can be attributed to a few key components.</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Find out which part has an error and mark it as your answer. If there is no error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</strong></p>

<p>It hardly oozes charm now, (A) / unless&nbsp;there have been (B) / a few attempts to gentrify it. (C) / No error (D)</p>

<p>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>My research focus on marine mammal behaviour, ecology and conservation.</p>

<p style=""><strong>The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</strong></p>

<p style="">One of the major challenges / he faced during his / undergraduate days were / the shortage of money.</p>

Of the four given options, choose the most appropriate one.
<div>&nbsp;</div>
options: <span style="-webkit-text-size-adjust: none;">Neither of the two men were strong enough to lift the weight.</span> /
&nbsp;<span style="-webkit-text-size-adjust: none;">&nbsp;Neither of the two men was stronger enough to lift the weight.</span> /
<span style="-webkit-text-size-adjust: none;">&nbsp;Neither of the two men was strong enough to lift the weight.</span> /
<span style="-webkit-text-size-adjust: none;">Neither of the two men was strong enough nor to lift the weight.</span> /
questions.explanation:
Here is the solution in the given HTML format:

The correct answer is 'B' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the plural form of the verb 'were' is correct.
  • In the given sentence, the subject 'two men' is plural.
  • When the subject is plural, the plural form of the verb should be used.
    • Example: The men were working hard.
  • Therefore, the plural form of the verb 'were' is used correctly in the sentence.
     

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Correct sentence: Neither of the two men was strong enough to lift the weight.

Additional Information

  • If the subject is singular and is connected by the words "neither/nor", the verb is singular.
    • Example: Neither he nor she is ready.
  • If the subject is plural and is connected by the words "neither/nor", the verb is plural.
    • Example: Neither the boys nor the girls are coming.
Note that the correct option is 3 as per the given data, and the explanation has been adapted to align with the correct usage of the verb forms and the context provided.
